Polynomial interpolation is a generalization of linear interpolation.

Generally, if we have n data points, there is exactly one polynomial of degree at most n −1 going through all the data points.

Spline interpolation uses low-degree polynomials in each of the intervals, and chooses the polynomial pieces such that they fit smoothly together.

mimetic interpolation evaluates the integral of fields on target lines, areas or volumes

annotations.label: Mimetic interpolation preserves vector calculus identities

The Theory of Functional Connections (TFC) is a mathematical framework specifically developed for functional interpolation .

trigonometric interpolation is interpolation by trigonometric polynomials using Fourier series

Multivariate interpolation is the interpolation of functions of more than one variable.

The term extrapolation is used to find data points outside the range of known data points.
In curve fitting problems, the constraint that the interpolant has to go exactly through the data points is relaxed.
